events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes hot work where the "permit scope exceeded" - meaning the work performed went beyond what was authorized in the hot work permit. This is a non-compliance issue under both NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J.

Key points:
- Hot work permit scope was exceeded
- Work is at rough-complete stage
- This is non-compliant - the permit defines the scope, fire watch, and safety measures for specific work
- Exceeding permit scope means work was done outside authorized parameters

Decision: fail
Risk: high (hot work outside permit scope is a serious fire/safety hazard)
Component conditions: non-compliant
Percent complete: ~75 (rough-complete)
Confidence: high

    "Hot work performed beyond the authorized scope of the issued hot work permit",
    "Permit scope exceeded — work conducted outside the boundaries, activities, or conditions approved on the permit"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all hot work immediately and secure the area",
    "Re-evaluate the work performed outside permit scope and assess for fire hazards or damage",
    "Issue a new or amended hot work permit covering the actual scope of work before resuming",
    "Conduct fire watch and area inspection for at least 30 minutes after work cessation per NFPA 51B",
    "Document the permit deviation and notify the safety officer and third-party inspector"
  ],
  "rationale": "Hot work permits define the specific scope, location, duration, and fire-prevention measures required. Performing work beyond the authorized permit scope violates NFPA 51B permit requirements and OSHA 1926 Subpart J fire-protection provisions, creating uncontrolled fire and personnel-safety hazards. The work is non-compliant at rough-complete stage and must be halted and re-permitted before proceeding.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B — Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J — Welding and Cutting, 29 CFR 1926.350–354"
  ]
}
